title: The Devotional Coherence of the Methods and the Meditation on Vishnu
summary: Krishna teaches Uddhava about witness consciousness, detachment from family/objects, and devotion (Bhakti). Addresses relationship endings, dealing with grief, letting go of attachments, and mental calm.

sections.summary: Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “S'r Uddhava said: 'Krishna, the defenders of the Absolute Truth speak of many processes for spiritual advancement, are they supreme in their…” through “With inevitably a beginning and an end to the meager ends gained with one's karma, there is the prospect of misery as a consequence; situated in…” through “The yoga system, analytical philosophy, pious activities, Vedic study, austerity and renunciation, oh Uddhava, do not keep Me as satisfied as a…”

Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “One obtains Me by single-minded devotion with faith in the Supreme Soul, the object of love of the devotees. Devotional service directed at Me…” through “S'r Uddhava said: 'Oh Lotus-eyed One, how should one meditate on You when one desires liberation, in what way and on what form? Please, can You…” through “With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards.…”

Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards.…” through “Thus being established, one should withdraw one's consciousness by meditating on the ether. Also forsaking that, one next must ascend to Me and…” through “The deluded state of considering oneself the owner, the knower and the doer, will all together soon find its dissolution in the mind of the yogi…”

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच वदन्ति कृष्ण श्रेयांसि बहूनि ब्रह्म-वादिनः । तेषां विकल्प-प्राधान्यम् उताहो एक-मुख्यता
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca vadanti kṛṣṇa śreyāṃsi bahūni brahma-vādinaḥ / teṣāṃ vikalpa-prādhānyam utāho eka-mukhyatā
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Krishna, the defenders of the Absolute Truth speak of many processes for spiritual advancement, are they supreme in their combination or would one of them be the most important?

verses.sanskrit: भवतोदाहृतः स्वामिन् भक्ति-योगो ऽनपेक्षितः । निरस्य सर्वतः सङ्गं येन त्वय्य् आविशेन् मनः
verses.transliteration: bhavatodāhṛtaḥ svāmin bhakti-yogo 'napekṣitaḥ / nirasya sarvataḥ saṅgaṃ yena tvayy āviśen manaḥ
verses.translation: You stated clearly, oh Master, that the yoga of devotion by which the mind becomes fixed on You, practiced free from desires, removes all material attachment.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच कालेन नष्टा प्रलये वाणीयं वेद-संज्ञिता । मयादौ ब्रह्मणे प्रोक्ता धर्मो यस्यां मद्-आत्मकः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca kālena naṣṭā pralaye vāṇīyaṃ veda-saṃjñitā / mayādau brahmaṇe proktā dharmo yasyāṃ mad-ātmakaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'This message known as the Vedas that concerns the dharma that is founded on Me, was lost at the time of the universal annihilation [pralaya], but was by Me at the beginning [of a new era] spoken to Brahm [see also 3.9: 29-43].

verses.sanskrit: तेन प्रोक्ता स्व-पुत्राय मनवे पूर्व-जाय सा । ततो भृग्व्-आदयो ऽगृह्णन् सप्त ब्रह्म-महर्षयः
verses.transliteration: tena proktā sva-putrāya manave pūrva-jāya sā / tato bhṛgv-ādayo 'gṛhṇan sapta brahma-maharṣayaḥ
verses.translation: Brahm spoke this to his eldest son Manu, and the seven great sages headed by Bhrigu in their turn accepted it from Manu [see 8.1 & 8.13 and B.G. 4: 1-3].

verses.sanskrit: तेभ्यः पितृभ्यस् तत्-पुत्रा देव-दानव-गुह्यकाः । मनुष्याः सिद्ध-गन्धर्वाः स-विद्याधर-चारणाः
verses.transliteration: tebhyaḥ pitṛbhyas tat-putrā deva-dānava-guhyakāḥ / manuṣyāḥ siddha-gandharvāḥ sa-vidyādhara-cāraṇāḥ
verses.translation: From these forefathers, there were the following descendants: the godly and the demoniac souls, the S'iva followers, the human beings, the perfected souls and the singers of heaven, the scientists and the venerable souls. From [different combinations of] rajas, tamas and sattva [the gunas] the many different natures generated of the different humanoids [Kimdevas], the half-humans [Kinnaras], the snake-like types [Ngas], the wild men [Rkshasas] and the ape-like souls [Kimpurushas]. From all these living entities, who by their propensities are divided in many types and just as many leaders, the diversity of rituals and mantras flowed [like rivers from a mountain].

verses.sanskrit: किन्देवाः किन्नरा नागा रक्षः-किम्पुरुषादयः । बह्व्यस् तेषां प्रकृतयो रजः-सत्त्व-तमो-भुवः
verses.transliteration: kindevāḥ kinnarā nāgā rakṣaḥ-kimpuruṣādayaḥ / bahvyas teṣāṃ prakṛtayo rajaḥ-sattva-tamo-bhuvaḥ
verses.translation: From these forefathers, there were the following descendants: the godly and the demoniac souls, the S'iva followers, the human beings, the perfected souls and the singers of heaven, the scientists and the venerable souls. From [different combinations of] rajas, tamas and sattva [the gunas] the many different natures generated of the different humanoids [Kimdevas], the half-humans [Kinnaras], the snake-like types [Ngas], the wild men [Rkshasas] and the ape-like souls [Kimpurushas]. From all these living entities, who by their propensities are divided in many types and just as many leaders, the diversity of rituals and mantras flowed [like rivers from a mountain].

verses.sanskrit: याभिर् भूतानि भिद्यन्ते भूतानां पतयस् तथा । यथा-प्रकृति सर्वेषां चित्रा वाचः स्रवन्ति हि
verses.transliteration: yābhir bhūtāni bhidyante bhūtānāṃ patayas tathā / yathā-prakṛti sarveṣāṃ citrā vācaḥ sravanti hi
verses.translation: From these forefathers, there were the following descendants: the godly and the demoniac souls, the S'iva followers, the human beings, the perfected souls and the singers of heaven, the scientists and the venerable souls. From [different combinations of] rajas, tamas and sattva [the gunas] the many different natures generated of the different humanoids [Kimdevas], the half-humans [Kinnaras], the snake-like types [Ngas], the wild men [Rkshasas] and the ape-like souls [Kimpurushas]. From all these living entities, who by their propensities are divided in many types and just as many leaders, the diversity of rituals and mantras flowed [like rivers from a mountain].

verses.sanskrit: एवं प्रकृति-वैचित्र्याद् भिद्यन्ते मतयो नृणाम् । पारम्पर्येण केषाञ्चित् पाषण्ड-मतयो ऽपरे
verses.transliteration: evaṃ prakṛti-vaicitryād bhidyante matayo nṛṇām / pāramparyeṇa keṣāñcit pāṣaṇḍa-matayo 'pare
verses.translation: Due to this great variety of natures the philosophies of life are thus divided among the human beings. Some of those philosophies in this constitute traditions of disciplic succession while others are heretical [pshanda].

verses.sanskrit: मन्-माया-मोहित-धियः पुरुषाः पुरुषर्षभ । श्रेयो वदन्त्य् अनेकान्तं यथा-कर्म यथा-रुचि
verses.transliteration: man-māyā-mohita-dhiyaḥ puruṣāḥ puruṣarṣabha / śreyo vadanty anekāntaṃ yathā-karma yathā-ruci
verses.translation: The people whose intelligence is bewildered by My illusory power, oh best of all persons, express themselves in countless ways about what according to their karma and taste would be better.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मम् एके यशश् चान्ये कामं सत्यं दमं शमम् । अन्ये वदन्ति स्वार्थं वा ऐश्वर्यं त्याग-भोजनम् । केचिद् यज्ञं तपो दानं व्रतानि नियमान् यमान्
verses.transliteration: dharmam eke yaśaś cānye kāmaṃ satyaṃ damaṃ śamam / anye vadanti svārthaṃ vā aiśvaryaṃ tyāga-bhojanam / kecid yajñaṃ tapo dānaṃ vratāni niyamān yamān
verses.translation: Some people speak in favor of pious activities while others speak of fame, sense gratification, truthfulness, self-control and peacefulness. Some propound self-interest, political influence, renunciation or consumption and others defend sacrifice, penance, charity, vows and arrangements of do's and don'ts [yama-niyama].

verses.sanskrit: आद्य्-अन्त-वन्त एवैषां लोकाः कर्म-विनिर्मिताः । दुःखोदर्कास् तमो-निष्ठाः क्षुद्रा मन्दाः शुचार्पिताः
verses.transliteration: ādy-anta-vanta evaiṣāṃ lokāḥ karma-vinirmitāḥ / duḥkhodarkās tamo-niṣṭhāḥ kṣudrā mandāḥ śucārpitāḥ
verses.translation: With inevitably a beginning and an end to the meager ends gained with one's karma, there is the prospect of misery as a consequence; situated in ignorance one is doomed to a miserable existence full of complaints.

verses.sanskrit: मय्य् अर्पितात्मनः सभ्य निरपेक्षस्य सर्वतः । मयात्मना सुखं यत् तत् कुतः स्याद् विषयात्मनाम्
verses.transliteration: mayy arpitātmanaḥ sabhya nirapekṣasya sarvataḥ / mayātmanā sukhaṃ yat tat kutaḥ syād viṣayātmanām
verses.translation: Someone who has fixed his consciousness on Me, oh man of learning, and in every respect is free from material desires, knows the happiness of My spiritual Self. How can such a happiness ever be attained by those who are attached to worldly affairs [see 4.31: 12]?

verses.sanskrit: अकिञ्चनस्य दान्तस्य शान्तस्य सम-चेतसः । मया सन्तुष्ट-मनसः सर्वाः सुख-मया दिशः
verses.transliteration: akiñcanasya dāntasya śāntasya sama-cetasaḥ / mayā santuṣṭa-manasaḥ sarvāḥ sukha-mayā diśaḥ
verses.translation: He who does not desire, who is of peace and controls his senses, whose consciousness is equal whatever the circumstances and who has a mind that is completely satisfied with Me, is filled with happiness wherever he goes.

verses.sanskrit: न पारमेष्ठ्यं न महेन्द्र-धिष्ण्यं न सार्वभौमं न रसाधिपत्यम् न योग-सिद्धीर् अपुनर्-भवं वा मय्य् अर्पितात्मेच्छति मद् विनान्यत्
verses.transliteration: na pārameṣṭhyaṃ na mahendra-dhiṣṇyaṃ $ na sārvabhaumaṃ na rasādhipatyam &na yoga-siddhīr apunar-bhavaṃ vā % mayy arpitātmecchati mad vinānyat
verses.translation: A soul fixed on Me, wants Me and nothing else. He does not desire the position of Brahm, nor the position of Indra. He neither wants an empire on earth nor a sovereign position in the lower worlds, he does not desire the perfections [the siddhis] of yoga or a second birth [see e.g. 5.1: 6].

verses.sanskrit: न तथा मे प्रियतम आत्म-योनिर् न शङ्करः । न च सङ्कर्षणो न श्रीर् नैवात्मा च यथा भवान्
verses.transliteration: na tathā me priyatama ātma-yonir na śaṅkaraḥ / na ca saṅkarṣaṇo na śrīr naivātmā ca yathā bhavān
verses.translation: Neither the one born from My body [Brahm], nor S'ankara [S'iva], Sankarshana [Balarma], the Goddess of Fortune [S'r] or even My own Self, is as dear to Me as you [see also B.G 12: 20].

verses.sanskrit: निरपेक्षं मुनिं शान्तं निर्वैरं सम-दर्शनम् । अनुव्रजाम्य् अहं नित्यं पूयेयेत्य् अङ्घ्रि-रेणुभिः
verses.transliteration: nirapekṣaṃ muniṃ śāntaṃ nirvairaṃ sama-darśanam / anuvrajāmy ahaṃ nityaṃ pūyeyety aṅghri-reṇubhiḥ
verses.translation: The sage [the devotee] who without personal desire is of peace, who is not inimical to anyone and of an equal vision, I always follow closely so that there is purification with the dust of the lotus feet [see also 7.14: 17].

verses.sanskrit: निष्किञ्चना मय्य् अनुरक्त-चेतसः शान्ता महान्तो ऽखिल-जीव-वत्सलाः । कामैर् अनालब्ध-धियो जुषन्ति ते यन् नैरपेक्ष्यं न विदुः सुखं मम
verses.transliteration: niṣkiñcanā mayy anurakta-cetasaḥ śāntā mahānto 'khila-jīva-vatsalāḥ / kāmair anālabdha-dhiyo juṣanti te yan nairapekṣyaṃ na viduḥ sukhaṃ mama
verses.translation: The great souls who, not eager for sense gratification, are of a mind that is constantly attached to Me, who are of inner peace, who care for all individual souls and whose consciousness is not ruled by lusts, experience My happiness that can only be known in complete independence [in detachment].

verses.sanskrit: बाध्यमानो ऽपि मद्-भक्तो विषयैर् अजितेन्द्रियः । प्रायः प्रगल्भया भक्त्या विषयैर् नाभिभूयते
verses.transliteration: bādhyamāno 'pi mad-bhakto viṣayair ajitendriyaḥ / prāyaḥ pragalbhayā bhaktyā viṣayair nābhibhūyate
verses.translation: Despite being harassed by sensual desires, a devotee of Mine who did not conquer his often strong senses, by dint of his devotion will not be defeated by them [see also 1.5: 17, 8.7: 44, 11.13: 12 and B.G. 9: 30, 2: 62 -64].

verses.sanskrit: यथाग्निः सु-समृद्धार्चिः करोत्य् एधांसि भस्मसात् । तथा मद्-विषया भक्तिर् उद्धवैनांसि कृत्स्नशः
verses.transliteration: yathāgniḥ su-samṛddhārciḥ karoty edhāṃsi bhasmasāt / tathā mad-viṣayā bhaktir uddhavaināṃsi kṛtsnaśaḥ
verses.translation: Just as firewood because of the blazing flames of a fire turns into ashes, devotion with Me as the object burns the [consequences of one's] sins completely, oh Uddhava.

verses.sanskrit: न साधयति मां योगो न साङ्ख्यं धर्म उद्धव । न स्वाध्यायस् तपस् त्यागो यथा भक्तिर् ममोर्जिता
verses.transliteration: na sādhayati māṃ yogo na sāṅkhyaṃ dharma uddhava / na svādhyāyas tapas tyāgo yathā bhaktir mamorjitā
verses.translation: The yoga system, analytical philosophy, pious activities, Vedic study, austerity and renunciation, oh Uddhava, do not keep Me as satisfied as a well developed devotional service unto Me.

verses.sanskrit: भक्त्याहम् एकया ग्राह्यः श्रद्धयात्मा प्रियः सताम् । भक्तिः पुनाति मन्-निष्ठा श्व-पाकान् अपि सम्भवात्
verses.transliteration: bhaktyāham ekayā grāhyaḥ śraddhayātmā priyaḥ satām / bhaktiḥ punāti man-niṣṭhā śva-pākān api sambhavāt
verses.translation: One obtains Me by single-minded devotion with faith in the Supreme Soul, the object of love of the devotees. Devotional service directed at Me will even purify someone who has eaten dogs.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मः सत्य-दयोपेतो विद्या वा तपसान्विता । मद्-भक्त्यापेतम् आत्मानं न सम्यक् प्रपुनाति हि
verses.transliteration: dharmaḥ satya-dayopeto vidyā vā tapasānvitā / mad-bhaktyāpetam ātmānaṃ na samyak prapunāti hi
verses.translation: Neither dharma accompanied by truthfulness and mercy, nor knowledge linked to austerity, will purify one's consciousness fully when bereft of devotional service unto Me.

verses.sanskrit: कथं विना रोम-हर्षं द्रवता चेतसा विना । विनानन्दाश्रु-कलया शुध्येद् भक्त्या विनाशयः
verses.transliteration: kathaṃ vinā roma-harṣaṃ dravatā cetasā vinā / vinānandāśru-kalayā śudhyed bhaktyā vināśayaḥ
verses.translation: How can without bhakti one's hair stand on end, without loving service the heart melt, without devotion the tears flow, the bliss be and one's heart be purified?

verses.sanskrit: वाग् गद्गदा द्रवते यस्य चित्तं रुदत्य् अभीक्ष्णं हसति क्वचिच् च । विलज्ज उद्गायति नृत्यते च मद्-भक्ति-युक्तो भुवनं पुनाति
verses.transliteration: vāg gadgadā dravate yasya cittaṃ rudaty abhīkṣṇaṃ hasati kvacic ca / vilajja udgāyati nṛtyate ca mad-bhakti-yukto bhuvanaṃ punāti
verses.translation: The universe is purified by the one whose voice chokes up, whose heart melts, who cries again and again, sometimes laughs, is ashamed, loudly sings and dances in the connectedness of My bhakti [see also S'r S'r S'ikshshthaka and 11.2: 40].

verses.sanskrit: यथाग्निना हेम मलं जहाति ध्मातं पुनः स्वं भजते च रूपम् । आत्मा च कर्मानुशयं विधूय मद्-भक्ति-योगेन भजत्य् अथो माम्
verses.transliteration: yathāgninā hema malaṃ jahāti dhmātaṃ punaḥ svaṃ bhajate ca rūpam / ātmā ca karmānuśayaṃ vidhūya mad-bhakti-yogena bhajaty atho mām
verses.translation: Just like gold being molten in fire gives up impurities and returns to its original state, also the contamination of karma is removed from the spirit soul when one worships Me united in My loving service.

verses.sanskrit: यथा यथात्मा परिमृज्यते ऽसौ मत्-पुण्य-गाथा-श्रवणाभिधानैः । तथा तथा पश्यति वस्तु सूक्ष्मं चक्षुर् यथैवाञ्जन-सम्प्रयुक्तम्
verses.transliteration: yathā yathātmā parimṛjyate 'sau mat-puṇya-gāthā-śravaṇābhidhānaiḥ / tathā tathā paśyati vastu sūkṣmaṃ cakṣur yathaivāñjana-samprayuktam
verses.translation: Even as the power to see restores once the eye is treated with ointment, the spirit soul again sees the One Subtle Essence once being cleansed by hearing and chanting My pious narrations.

verses.sanskrit: विषयान् ध्यायतश् चित्तं विषयेषु विषज्जते । माम् अनुस्मरतश् चित्तं मय्य् एव प्रविलीयते
verses.transliteration: viṣayān dhyāyataś cittaṃ viṣayeṣu viṣajjate / mām anusmarataś cittaṃ mayy eva pravilīyate
verses.translation: Just as the intelligence of someone meditating the objects of the senses is entangled in the sense experience [see B.G. 2: 62-63], one's thought becomes dissolved in Me when one keeps thinking of Me.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद् असद्-अभिध्यानं यथा स्वप्न-मनोरथम् । हित्वा मयि समाधत्स्व मनो मद्-भाव-भावितम्
verses.transliteration: tasmād asad-abhidhyānaṃ yathā svapna-manoratham / hitvā mayi samādhatsva mano mad-bhāva-bhāvitam
verses.translation: One's material considerations are therefore like the figments of a dream; [having awakened being] absorbed in Me one forsakes them. The mind is purified when one is completely absorbed in My love.

verses.sanskrit: स्त्रीणां स्त्री-सङ्गिनां सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा दूरत आत्मवान् । क्षेमे विविक्त आसीनश् चिन्तयेन् माम् अतन्द्रितः
verses.transliteration: strīṇāṃ strī-saṅgināṃ saṅgaṃ tyaktvā dūrata ātmavān / kṣeme vivikta āsīnaś cintayen mām atandritaḥ
verses.translation: Giving up being associated [intimately] with women and keeping away from those who chase women, one should [thus free from being physically identified] composed sit at ease in seclusion and with great care concentrate on Me [see also 11.8: 13-14 *].

verses.sanskrit: न तथास्य भवेत् क्लेशो बन्धश् चान्य-प्रसङ्गतः । योषित्-सङ्गाद् यथा पुंसो यथा तत्-सङ्गि-सङ्गतः
verses.transliteration: na tathāsya bhavet kleśo bandhaś cānya-prasaṅgataḥ / yoṣit-saṅgād yathā puṃso yathā tat-saṅgi-saṅgataḥ
verses.translation: No other attachment brings a man as much suffering and bondage as the attachment to women and the association with those who are attached to women [see also 1.4: 25, 5.5: 2, 5.13: 16, 6.9: 9, 7.12: 9, 9.14: 36, 9.19: 17, 10.10: 8, 10.51: 51, 10.60: 44-45 & 48].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच यथा त्वाम् अरविन्दाक्ष यादृशं वा यद्-आत्मकम् । ध्यायेन् मुमुक्षुर् एतन् मे ध्यानं त्वं वक्तुम् अर्हसि
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca yathā tvām aravindākṣa yādṛśaṃ vā yad-ātmakam / dhyāyen mumukṣur etan me dhyānaṃ tvaṃ vaktum arhasi
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Oh Lotus-eyed One, how should one meditate on You when one desires liberation, in what way and on what form? Please, can You speak to me about meditation?'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच सम आसन आसीनः सम-कायो यथा-सुखम् । हस्ताव् उत्सङ्ग आधाय स्व-नासाग्र-कृतेक्षणः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca sama āsana āsīnaḥ sama-kāyo yathā-sukham / hastāv utsaṅga ādhāya sva-nāsāgra-kṛtekṣaṇaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Sitting straight and comfortably on a seat at the level of the floor, one should place one's hands in one's lap and focus one's eyes on the tip of one's nose. Then one should clear the path of one's prna, one's vital air, step by step exercising inhaling, retaining and exhaling and the other way around - while controlling one's senses [**, see prnyma, and B.G. 4: 29].

verses.sanskrit: प्राणस्य शोधयेन् मार्गं पूर-कुम्भक-रेचकैः । विपर्ययेणापि शनैर् अभ्यसेन् निर्जितेन्द्रियः
verses.transliteration: prāṇasya śodhayen mārgaṃ pūra-kumbhaka-recakaiḥ / viparyayeṇāpi śanair abhyasen nirjitendriyaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'Sitting straight and comfortably on a seat at the level of the floor, one should place one's hands in one's lap and focus one's eyes on the tip of one's nose. Then one should clear the path of one's prna, one's vital air, step by step exercising inhaling, retaining and exhaling and the other way around - while controlling one's senses [**, see prnyma, and B.G. 4: 29].

verses.sanskrit: हृद्य् अविच्छिनम् ॐकारं घण्टा-नादं बिसोर्ण-वत् । प्राणेनोदीर्य तत्राथ पुनः संवेशयेत् स्वरम्
verses.transliteration: hṛdy avicchinam oṃkāraṃ ghaṇṭā-nādaṃ bisorṇa-vat / prāṇenodīrya tatrātha punaḥ saṃveśayet svaram
verses.translation: With the help of one's life breath [prna] one should, like through the fibre of a lotus stalk, guide the sound AUM upwards to the heart, to vibrate it there continuously like a ringing bell so as to reunite the [15 after] sounds of recitation [in the nose, anusvra ***].

verses.sanskrit: एवं प्रणव-संयुक्तं प्राणम् एव समभ्यसेत् । दश-कृत्वस् त्रि-षवणं मासाद् अर्वाग् जितानिलः
verses.transliteration: evaṃ praṇava-saṃyuktaṃ prāṇam eva samabhyaset / daśa-kṛtvas tri-ṣavaṇaṃ māsād arvāg jitānilaḥ
verses.translation: The life breath thus joined with the Pranava [see also 9.14: 46] must be practiced ten times, at sunrise, noon and sunset. After one month one will then be in control of one's vital air [*4].

verses.sanskrit: हृत्-पुण्डरीकम् अन्तः-स्थम् ऊर्ध्व-नालम् अधो-मुखम् । ध्यात्वोर्ध्व-मुखम् उन्निद्रम् अष्ट-पत्रं स-कर्णिकम्
verses.transliteration: hṛt-puṇḍarīkam antaḥ-stham ūrdhva-nālam adho-mukham / dhyātvordhva-mukham unnidram aṣṭa-patraṃ sa-karṇikam
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: कर्णिकायां न्यसेत् सूर्य- सोमाग्नीन् उत्तरोत्तरम् । वह्नि-मध्ये स्मरेद् रूपं ममैतद् ध्यान-मङ्गलम्
verses.transliteration: karṇikāyāṃ nyaset sūrya- somāgnīn uttarottaram / vahni-madhye smared rūpaṃ mamaitad dhyāna-maṅgalam
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: समं प्रशान्तं सु-मुखं दीर्घ-चारु-चतुर्-भुजम् । सु-चारु-सुन्दर-ग्रीवं सु-कपोलं शुचि-स्मितम्
verses.transliteration: samaṃ praśāntaṃ su-mukhaṃ dīrgha-cāru-catur-bhujam / su-cāru-sundara-grīvaṃ su-kapolaṃ śuci-smitam
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: समान-कर्ण-विन्यस्त- स्फुरन्-मकर-कुण्डलम् । हेमाम्बरं घन-श्यामं श्रीवत्स-श्री-निकेतनम्
verses.transliteration: samāna-karṇa-vinyasta- sphuran-makara-kuṇḍalam / hemāmbaraṃ ghana-śyāmaṃ śrīvatsa-śrī-niketanam
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: शङ्ख-चक्र-गदा-पद्म- वनमाला-विभूषितम् । नूपुरैर् विलसत्-पादं कौस्तुभ-प्रभया युतम्
verses.transliteration: śaṅkha-cakra-gadā-padma- vanamālā-vibhūṣitam / nūpurair vilasat-pādaṃ kaustubha-prabhayā yutam
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: द्युमत्-किरीट-कटक- कटि-सूत्राङ्गदायुतम् । सर्वाङ्ग-सुन्दरं हृद्यं प्रसाद-सुमुखेक्षनम्
verses.transliteration: dyumat-kirīṭa-kaṭaka- kaṭi-sūtrāṅgadāyutam / sarvāṅga-sundaraṃ hṛdyaṃ prasāda-sumukhekṣanam
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: सु-कुमारम् अभिध्यायेत् सर्वाङ्गेषु मनो दधत् । इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यो मनसाकृष्य तन् मनः । बुद्ध्या सारथिना धीरः प्रणयेन् मयि सर्वतः
verses.transliteration: su-kumāram abhidhyāyet sarvāṅgeṣu mano dadhat / indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyo manasākṛṣya tan manaḥ / buddhyā sārathinā dhīraḥ praṇayen mayi sarvataḥ
verses.translation: With one's eyes half closed one should, alert and with one's face lifted, concentrate on the lotus within the heart that is directed upwards. Within the whorl of its eight petals one should one after the other picture the sun, the moon and the fire. Within the fire one should consider My harmonious form, so auspicious for meditation, that is gentle, cheerful and endowed with four beautiful arms. The neck and the forehead are of a charming beauty as also the pure smile and the ears with the glowing shark shaped earrings. One should meditate on the golden dress, the complexion with the color of a rain cloud, the curl on the chest where the goddess resides, the conch and the disc, the club and the lotus, as also on the beauty of the forest flower garland. One should also meditate on all the beautiful and charming parts of My body: the feet with their shining bells, the richly glowing Kaustubha gem, the shining crown and bracelets, the girdle and armlets, the merciful smile and the delicate glance. By turning the mind away from the senses and their sense objects, one thus with intelligence should lead the charioteer of one's thinking [the soul, the master of intelligence], sober and grave, [with love] in the direction of My completeness.

verses.sanskrit: तत् सर्व-व्यापकं चित्तम् आकृष्यैकत्र धारयेत् । नान्यानि चिन्तयेद् भूयः सु-स्मितं भावयेन् मुखम्
verses.transliteration: tat sarva-vyāpakaṃ cittam ākṛṣyaikatra dhārayet / nānyāni cintayed bhūyaḥ su-smitaṃ bhāvayen mukham
verses.translation: One's consciousness spread over all of this then must be withdrawn to one point by focussing on the wonderful smile of the face, and by no longer regarding other parts.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र लब्ध-पदं चित्तम् आकृष्य व्योम्नि धारयेत् । तच् च त्यक्त्वा मद्-आरोहो न किञ्चिद् अपि चिन्तयेत्
verses.transliteration: tatra labdha-padaṃ cittam ākṛṣya vyomni dhārayet / tac ca tyaktvā mad-āroho na kiñcid api cintayet
verses.translation: Thus being established, one should withdraw one's consciousness by meditating on the ether. Also forsaking that, one next must ascend to Me and not think of anything else anymore.

verses.sanskrit: एवं समाहित-मतिर् माम् एवात्मानम् आत्मनि । विचष्टे मयि सर्वात्मन् ज्योतिर् ज्योतिषि संयुतम्
verses.transliteration: evaṃ samāhita-matir mām evātmānam ātmani / vicaṣṭe mayi sarvātman jyotir jyotiṣi saṃyutam
verses.translation: With one's consciousness thus fully absorbed, the individual soul sees Me in the self and all selves in Me, the same way the rays of the sun are united in the sun [see also B.G. 9: 29].

verses.sanskrit: ध्यानेनेत्थं सु-तीव्रेण युञ्जतो योगिनो मनः । संयास्यत्य् आशु निर्वाणं द्रव्य ज्ञान-क्रिया-भ्रमः
verses.transliteration: dhyānenetthaṃ su-tīvreṇa yuñjato yogino manaḥ / saṃyāsyaty āśu nirvāṇaṃ dravya jñāna-kriyā-bhramaḥ
verses.translation: The deluded state of considering oneself the owner, the knower and the doer, will all together soon find its dissolution in the mind of the yogi who, most concentrated, practices the meditation as mentioned [compare 2.2: 8-14].'
